---
title: "HERA_08a_Explore_Single_GAMs"
author: "Tory Eisenlohr-Moul"
date: "2026-03-19"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(scales)
```



```{r}
# Load packages, set options, create output folder
source("HERA_00_setup.R")

# Create subfolder for data prep outputs
gam_exp_folder <- file.path(output_folder, "08a_GAM_exploration")
if (!dir.exists(gam_exp_folder)) {
  dir.create(gam_exp_folder, recursive = TRUE)
  cat("Created subfolder:", gam_exp_folder, "\n")
}
```



```{r load-data}


gam_df <- read.csv("~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/HERA/03_analytic_projects/HERA-Primary ms/02_datasets_output/06_Single_GAMs/GAM_Reports_20260319/participant_significance_summary_20260319.csv")
```

```{r load-raw-data}
# --- Load raw dataset for fitting individual GAMs per ID ---
# Update this path to match your local data file
raw_data_path <- "data/HERA_20260312_clean.rds"
if (file.exists(raw_data_path)) {
  df_raw <- readRDS(raw_data_path)
  cat("Loaded raw data:", nrow(df_raw), "rows,", ncol(df_raw), "columns\n")
} else {
  stop("Raw data file not found at: ", raw_data_path,
       "\nPlease update the path to your clean RDS file.")
}
```

```{r define-outcomes-and-gam-setup}
# --- Define Outcomes to Model (must match HERA_08_single_GAMs.Rmd) ---
outcomes <- c(
  "drsp_NA"          = "DRSP Negative Affect Mean",
  "drsp_NAmax"       = "DRSP Negative Affect Max",
  "drsp_total"       = "DRSP Total",
  "drsp_distress"    = "Distress (DRSP)",
  "drsp_labilsocsx"  = "Social/Affect Lability (DRSP)",
  "drsp_breastpain"  = "Breast Sx (DRSP)",
  "drsp_eatsx"       = "Eating Sx",
  "drsp_lowarousal"  = "Low Arousal Sx (DRSP)",
  "drsp_1"           = "Depression",
  "drsp_2"           = "Hopelessness",
  "drsp_3"           = "Worthlessness",
  "drsp_4"           = "Anxiety",
  "drsp_5"           = "Mood Swings",
  "drsp_6"           = "Rejection Sensitivity",
  "drsp_7"           = "Anger/Irritability",
  "drsp_8"           = "Interpersonal Conflicts",
  "drsp_9"           = "Loss of Interest",
  "drsp_16"          = "Feeling Overwhelmed",
  "drsp_17"          = "Feeling Out of Control",
  "drsp_10"          = "Difficulty Concentrating",
  "drsp_11"          = "Fatigue/Lethargy",
  "drsp_14"          = "Hypersomnia",
  "drsp_15"          = "Insomnia",
  "drsp_12"          = "Overeating",
  "drsp_13"          = "Specific Food Cravings",
  "drsp_18"          = "Breast Tenderness",
  "drsp_19"          = "Breast Swelling",
  "drsp_20"          = "Headache",
  "drsp_21"          = "Joint/Muscle Pain"
)

# --- Time Variable for individual GAM fitting ---
time_vars_to_run <- c("cyclic_time_impute")
time_vars <- intersect(time_vars_to_run, names(df_raw))
if (length(time_vars) == 0) {
  warning("No matching time variables found in raw data. Individual GAM plots will be skipped.")
}

# --- Pre-calculate log-transformations on raw data ---
for (var in names(outcomes)) {
  if (var %in% names(df_raw)) {
    df_raw[[paste0(var, "_Log")]] <- log(df_raw[[var]] + 1)
  }
}

# --- Prediction grids reused per time var ---
pred_grid_list <- setNames(lapply(time_vars, function(tv) {
  d <- data.frame(seq(-1, 1, by = 0.05))
  names(d) <- tv
  d
}), time_vars)

# --- Axis labels + reference lines per time var ---
# Time variables containing "ov" are ovulation-centered (ovulation = 0);
# otherwise menses-centered (menses = 0). This affects label order and
# which phase reference lines are drawn.
axis_meta <- lapply(time_vars, function(tv) {
  if (grepl("ov", tv, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    list(labels = c("Menses", "50%F", "Ovulation", "50%L", "Menses"),
         menses = c(-1, 1), ov = 0)
  } else {
    list(labels = c("Ovulation", "50%L", "Menses", "50%F", "Ovulation"),
         menses = 0, ov = c(-1, 1))
  }
})
names(axis_meta) <- time_vars
```

```{r}
names(gam_df)

# Calculate Significance (must happen before any reference to gam_df$significant)
gam_df <- gam_df %>%
  mutate(significant = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, "Significant", "Not Significant"))

unique(gam_df$significant)
```


```{r}
# make it again but sort the bars by the number of significant participants
df_summary <- gam_df %>%
  group_by(variable) %>%
  summarise(significant_count = sum(significant == "Significant")) %>%
  arrange(desc(significant_count))

ggplot(df_summary, aes(x = reorder(variable, -significant_count), y = significant_count)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Number of Participants with Significant Effects by Outcome", x = "Outcome",
       y = "Count of Significant Participants") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))


```


```{r}
# plot the distribution of range among ids with and without significance 

ggplot(gam_df, aes(x = significant, y = range)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of DRSP_NA Range by Significance", x = "Significance", y = "Range") +
  theme_minimal()

# now with histograms

ggplot(gam_df, aes(x = range, fill = significant)) +
  geom_histogram(position = "dodge", bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "Histogram of DRSP_NA Range by Significance", x = "Range", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue", "salmon")) +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank())
```
```{r}

# =========================================================
# PERSON-SPECIFIC GAM CYCLICITY SUMMARY AND THRESHOLD PLOTS
# =========================================================
# Expected variables in data:
# "id" "variable" "outcome_label" "p.value" "edf" "n"
# "range" "N" "significant"
#
# What this code does:
# 1. Cleans significance variable
# 2. Filters to one DRSP outcome (e.g., "drsp_NA")
# 3. Summarizes range by significance
# 4. Fits logistic models predicting GAM significance from range
#    (optionally controlling for n)
# 5. Plots probability of significance across range
# 6. Computes summary stats at candidate thresholds
# 7. Lets you run the same workflow across many DRSP variables
#
# Packages: loaded in setup chunk above (tidyverse, broom, scales)
# Data: loaded in load-data chunk above as gam_df

# =========================================================
# 2. BASIC CLEANING
# =========================================================
clean_gam_summary <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      significant_num = ifelse(significant == "Significant", 1, 0),
      significant_f = factor(significant_num, 
                             levels = c(0, 1), 
                             labels = c("Not Significant", "Significant")),
      range = as.numeric(range),
      n = as.numeric(n),
      variable = as.character(variable),
      id = as.character(id)
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(range), !is.na(significant_num), !is.na(variable), !is.na(id))
}

# =========================================================
# 3. FILTER TO ONE OUTCOME
# =========================================================
# Change target_var to whatever DRSP variable you want
target_var <- "drsp_NA"

get_outcome_df <- function(df, target_var) {
  df %>%
    filter(variable == target_var) %>%
    distinct(id, variable, .keep_all = TRUE)
}

# =========================================================
# 4. DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY FUNCTION
# =========================================================
summarize_cyclicity <- function(df_outcome) {
  overall <- df_outcome %>%
    summarise(
      n_participants = n(),
      n_significant = sum(significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      prop_significant = mean(significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  
  by_sig <- df_outcome %>%
    group_by(significant_f) %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      mean_range = mean(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_range = sd(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_range = median(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      q25_range = quantile(range, .25, na.rm = TRUE),
      q75_range = quantile(range, .75, na.rm = TRUE),
      min_range = min(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      max_range = max(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_n = mean(n, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  list(overall = overall, by_significance = by_sig)
}

# =========================================================
# 5. THRESHOLD TABLE
# =========================================================
threshold_table <- function(df_outcome,
                            thresholds = c(0.10, 0.25, 0.36, 0.50, 0.75, 1.00)) {
  map_dfr(thresholds, function(th) {
    tmp <- df_outcome %>% filter(range >= th)
    
    tibble(
      threshold = th,
      n_at_or_above = nrow(tmp),
      prop_of_sample = ifelse(nrow(df_outcome) > 0, nrow(tmp) / nrow(df_outcome), NA_real_),
      n_significant = sum(tmp$significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      prop_significant = ifelse(nrow(tmp) > 0, mean(tmp$significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE), NA_real_)
    )
  })
}

# =========================================================
# 6. LOGISTIC MODELS
# =========================================================
fit_logistic_models <- function(df_outcome) {
  model_range <- glm(significant_num ~ range, family = binomial, data = df_outcome)
  model_range_n <- glm(significant_num ~ range + n, family = binomial, data = df_outcome)
  
  list(
    model_range = model_range,
    model_range_n = model_range_n,
    tidy_range = broom::tidy(model_range, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE),
    tidy_range_n = broom::tidy(model_range_n, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE)
  )
}

# =========================================================
# 7. PREDICTION DATA FOR PLOTTING
# =========================================================
make_prediction_df <- function(df_outcome, model_range, model_range_n = NULL, n_value = NULL) {
  xseq <- seq(min(df_outcome$range, na.rm = TRUE),
              max(df_outcome$range, na.rm = TRUE),
              length.out = 300)
  
  pred1 <- tibble(range = xseq) %>%
    mutate(
      pred_prob = predict(model_range, newdata = ., type = "response"),
      model = "Range only"
    )
  
  if (!is.null(model_range_n)) {
    if (is.null(n_value)) n_value <- median(df_outcome$n, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    pred2 <- tibble(range = xseq, n = n_value) %>%
      mutate(
        pred_prob = predict(model_range_n, newdata = ., type = "response"),
        model = paste0("Range + n (n fixed at ", round(n_value, 1), ")")
      ) %>%
      select(range, pred_prob, model)
    
    bind_rows(pred1, pred2)
  } else {
    pred1
  }
}

# =========================================================
# 8. PLOT: HISTOGRAM + LOGISTIC CURVE
# =========================================================
plot_range_histogram <- function(df_outcome) {
  ggplot(df_outcome, aes(x = range, fill = significant_f)) +
    geom_histogram(position = "identity", alpha = 0.65, bins = 30) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Histogram of ", unique(df_outcome$variable), " Range by Significance"),
      x = "Cyclical range",
      y = "Count",
      fill = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16)
}

plot_significance_probability <- function(df_outcome,
                                          pred_df,
                                          thresholds = c(0.25, 0.36, 0.50),
                                          jitter_height = 0.04) {
  ggplot(df_outcome, aes(x = range, y = significant_num)) +
    geom_jitter(
      aes(color = significant_f),
      width = 0,
      height = jitter_height,
      alpha = 0.45,
      size = 2
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = pred_df,
      aes(x = range, y = pred_prob, linetype = model),
      linewidth = 1.2,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_vline(
      xintercept = thresholds,
      linetype = "dashed",
      alpha = 0.6
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      limits = c(-0.02, 1.02),
      breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1),
      labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Probability of Significant Cyclicity: ", unique(df_outcome$variable)),
      x = "Cyclical range",
      y = "Predicted probability of significant GAM",
      color = NULL,
      linetype = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16)
}

# =========================================================
# 9. OPTIONAL: EMPIRICAL BINNED PROBABILITY PLOT
# =========================================================
# This shows the observed proportion significant within bins of range.
make_empirical_bins <- function(df_outcome, n_bins = 10) {
  df_outcome %>%
    mutate(range_bin = ntile(range, n_bins)) %>%
    group_by(range_bin) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_range = mean(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      prop_significant = mean(significant_num, na.rm = TRUE),
      n = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

plot_empirical_and_model <- function(df_outcome, pred_df, empirical_df,
                                     thresholds = c(0.25, 0.36, 0.50)) {
  ggplot() +
    geom_point(
      data = empirical_df,
      aes(x = mean_range, y = prop_significant, size = n),
      alpha = 0.8
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = pred_df,
      aes(x = range, y = pred_prob, linetype = model),
      linewidth = 1.2
    ) +
    geom_vline(
      xintercept = thresholds,
      linetype = "dashed",
      alpha = 0.6
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      limits = c(0, 1),
      labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Empirical and Modeled Probability: ", unique(df_outcome$variable)),
      x = "Cyclical range",
      y = "Probability of significant GAM",
      size = "Bin n",
      linetype = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16)
}

# =========================================================
# 10. ALL-IN-ONE ANALYSIS FOR ONE VARIABLE
# =========================================================
analyze_one_outcome <- function(df, target_var,
                                thresholds = c(0.25, 0.36, 0.50),
                                n_bins = 10) {
  df_clean <- clean_gam_summary(df)
  df_outcome <- get_outcome_df(df_clean, target_var)
  
  if (nrow(df_outcome) == 0) {
    stop(paste("No rows found for variable:", target_var))
  }
  
  desc <- summarize_cyclicity(df_outcome)
  thresh_tab <- threshold_table(df_outcome, thresholds = thresholds)
  mods <- fit_logistic_models(df_outcome)
  pred_df <- make_prediction_df(
    df_outcome = df_outcome,
    model_range = mods$model_range,
    model_range_n = mods$model_range_n
  )
  emp_df <- make_empirical_bins(df_outcome, n_bins = n_bins)
  
  p_hist <- plot_range_histogram(df_outcome)
  p_prob <- plot_significance_probability(df_outcome, pred_df, thresholds = thresholds)
  p_emp <- plot_empirical_and_model(df_outcome, pred_df, emp_df, thresholds = thresholds)
  
  list(
    data = df_outcome,
    descriptives_overall = desc$overall,
    descriptives_by_significance = desc$by_significance,
    threshold_table = thresh_tab,
    model_range = mods$model_range,
    model_range_n = mods$model_range_n,
    model_range_tidy_OR = mods$tidy_range,
    model_range_n_tidy_OR = mods$tidy_range_n,
    predictions = pred_df,
    empirical_bins = emp_df,
    plot_histogram = p_hist,
    plot_probability = p_prob,
    plot_empirical = p_emp
  )
}

# =========================================================
# 11. RUN IT FOR DRSP_NA
# =========================================================
# Replace gam_df with your actual dataset object name
# result_drsp_na <- analyze_one_outcome(gam_df, target_var = "drsp_NA")

# Then inspect:
# result_drsp_na$descriptives_overall
# result_drsp_na$descriptives_by_significance
# result_drsp_na$threshold_table
# result_drsp_na$model_range_tidy_OR
# result_drsp_na$model_range_n_tidy_OR
# result_drsp_na$plot_histogram
# result_drsp_na$plot_probability
# result_drsp_na$plot_empirical

# =========================================================
# 12. SAVE PLOTS IF YOU WANT
# =========================================================
# ggsave("drsp_NA_histogram_by_significance.png", result_drsp_na$plot_histogram, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
# ggsave("drsp_NA_probability_of_significance.png", result_drsp_na$plot_probability, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
# ggsave("drsp_NA_empirical_probability.png", result_drsp_na$plot_empirical, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# =========================================================
# 13. RUN ACROSS ALL DRSP VARIABLES
# =========================================================
get_drsp_variables <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    clean_gam_summary() %>%
    distinct(variable) %>%
    pull(variable) %>%
    .[str_detect(., regex("^drsp", ignore_case = TRUE))]
}

summarize_all_drsp_vars <- function(df,
                                    thresholds = c(0.25, 0.36, 0.50)) {
  df_clean <- clean_gam_summary(df)
  drsp_vars <- get_drsp_variables(df_clean)
  
  map_dfr(drsp_vars, function(v) {
    df_v <- get_outcome_df(df_clean, v)
    
    if (nrow(df_v) < 5) return(NULL)
    
    mod1 <- try(glm(significant_num ~ range, family = binomial, data = df_v), silent = TRUE)
    mod2 <- try(glm(significant_num ~ range + n, family = binomial, data = df_v), silent = TRUE)
    
    th_tab <- threshold_table(df_v, thresholds = thresholds) %>%
      pivot_wider(
        names_from = threshold,
        values_from = c(n_at_or_above, prop_of_sample, n_significant, prop_significant),
        names_sep = "_th_"
      )
    
    out <- tibble(
      variable = v,
      n_participants = nrow(df_v),
      n_significant = sum(df_v$significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      prop_significant = mean(df_v$significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_range = median(df_v$range, na.rm = TRUE),
      q25_range = quantile(df_v$range, .25, na.rm = TRUE),
      q75_range = quantile(df_v$range, .75, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_n = mean(df_v$n, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
    
    if (!inherits(mod1, "try-error")) {
      tidy1 <- broom::tidy(mod1, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
        filter(term == "range") %>%
        transmute(
          OR_range = estimate,
          OR_range_low = conf.low,
          OR_range_high = conf.high,
          p_range = p.value
        )
      out <- bind_cols(out, tidy1)
    }
    
    if (!inherits(mod2, "try-error")) {
      tidy2 <- broom::tidy(mod2, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE)
      
      range_row <- tidy2 %>%
        filter(term == "range") %>%
        transmute(
          OR_range_adj_n = estimate,
          OR_range_adj_n_low = conf.low,
          OR_range_adj_n_high = conf.high,
          p_range_adj_n = p.value
        )
      
      n_row <- tidy2 %>%
        filter(term == "n") %>%
        transmute(
          OR_n = estimate,
          OR_n_low = conf.low,
          OR_n_high = conf.high,
          p_n = p.value
        )
      
      out <- bind_cols(out, range_row, n_row)
    }
    
    bind_cols(out, th_tab)
  }) %>%
    arrange(desc(prop_significant), desc(median_range))
}

# Example:
# all_drsp_summary <- summarize_all_drsp_vars(gam_df)
# View(all_drsp_summary)

# =========================================================
# 14. OPTIONAL: FIND THE 50% CROSSING FROM THE SIMPLE MODEL
# =========================================================
# This is not a "natural breakpoint"; it is just the range value
# where predicted probability of significance = 0.50.
get_p50_threshold <- function(model_range) {
  coefs <- coef(model_range)
  if (length(coefs) < 2 || is.na(coefs[1]) || is.na(coefs[2]) || coefs[2] == 0) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  -coefs[1] / coefs[2]
}

# Example:
# p50_drsp_na <- get_p50_threshold(result_drsp_na$model_range)
# p50_drsp_na

# =========================================================
# 15. VERY SHORT MINIMAL VERSION FOR JUST drsp_NA
# =========================================================
# Uncomment and run this if you want the leanest version only.
#
# df_clean <- clean_gam_summary(gam_df)
# df_na <- df_clean %>% filter(variable == "drsp_NA")
#
# m1 <- glm(significant_num ~ range, family = binomial, data = df_na)
# m2 <- glm(significant_num ~ range + n, family = binomial, data = df_na)
#
# pred_df <- make_prediction_df(df_na, m1, m2)
#
# ggplot(df_na, aes(range, significant_num)) +
#   geom_jitter(aes(color = significant_f), height = 0.04, width = 0, alpha = 0.45) +
#   geom_line(data = pred_df, aes(y = pred_prob, linetype = model), linewidth = 1.2) +
#   geom_vline(xintercept = c(0.25, 0.36, 0.50), linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.6) +
#   scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
#   labs(
#     title = "Probability of Significant Cyclicity: drsp_NA",
#     x = "Cyclical range",
#     y = "Probability of significant GAM",
#     color = NULL,
#     linetype = NULL
#   )


```

```{r gam-helper-functions}
# =========================================================
# GAM FITTING & PLOTTING FUNCTIONS (from HERA_08_single_GAMs.Rmd)
# These are used to generate individual GAM trajectory plots
# for qualifying IDs in the per-outcome exploration PDFs.
# =========================================================

# Fit a cyclic GAM for one outcome and one time variable on a single ID's data.
# Returns list(pred = predictions_df, sig = significance_tibble) or NULL on failure.
fit_outcome <- function(dat, outcome_var, tv, outcomes_map, k_max = 10L) {
  log_out <- paste0(outcome_var, "_Log")
  keep <- c(log_out, tv)
  if (!all(keep %in% names(dat))) return(NULL)
  dat <- dat[complete.cases(dat[, keep]), keep, drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(dat) < 10 || var(dat[[log_out]], na.rm = TRUE) < 1e-6) return(NULL)

  k <- min(k_max, nrow(dat) - 1L); if (k < 3L) return(NULL)

  fml <- as.formula(paste0(log_out, " ~ s(", tv, ", bs = 'cc', k = ", k, ")"))
  knots <- setNames(list(c(-1, 1)), tv)

  mf <- try(mgcv::bam(fml, data = dat, knots = knots, method = "fREML",
                      discrete = TRUE, select = TRUE),
            silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(mf, "try-error")) return(NULL)

  s  <- summary(mf)
  p  <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(s$s.pv[1]))
  st <- s$s.table
  edf <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(st[1, "edf"]))
  Fval <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(st[1, "F"]))

  pg <- pred_grid_list[[tv]]
  pr <- predict(mf, newdata = pg, se.fit = TRUE, type = "link")
  est <- exp(pr$fit) - 1
  conf.low  <- exp(pr$fit - 1.96 * pr$se.fit) - 1
  conf.high <- exp(pr$fit + 1.96 * pr$se.fit) - 1

  pred <- cbind(pg, estimate = est, conf.low = conf.low, conf.high = conf.high)
  pred$outcome_label <- outcomes_map[[outcome_var]]
  pred$p.value <- p
  pred$raw_range <- diff(range(est, na.rm = TRUE))

  list(
    pred = pred,
    sig  = tibble::tibble(
      variable = outcome_var,
      outcome_label = outcomes_map[[outcome_var]],
      time_var = tv,
      p.value = p,
      edf = edf,
      F = Fval,
      n = nrow(dat),
      range = diff(range(est, na.rm = TRUE))
    )
  )
}

# Create an annotated ggplot of predicted GAM trajectory with CI ribbon,
# peak/trough points, range annotation, and phase reference lines.
# plot_df must contain: estimate, conf.low, conf.high, outcome_label, p.value, raw_range.
# axis_meta_list must have per-tv entries with: labels, menses, ov.
create_annotated_plot <- function(plot_df, tv, axis_meta_list) {
  if (is.null(plot_df) || nrow(plot_df) == 0) return(NULL)
  max_pt <- dplyr::slice_max(plot_df, order_by = estimate, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)
  min_pt <- dplyr::slice_min(plot_df, order_by = estimate, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)
  rng   <- dplyr::first(plot_df$raw_range)
  p_val <- dplyr::first(plot_df$p.value)
  stars <- dplyr::case_when(
    is.finite(p_val) & p_val < 0.001 ~ "***",
    is.finite(p_val) & p_val < 0.01  ~ "**",
    is.finite(p_val) & p_val < 0.05  ~ "*",
    TRUE ~ ""
  )
  ann   <- sprintf("%s Range = %.2f", stars, rng)
  meta  <- axis_meta_list[[tv]]

  ggplot2::ggplot(plot_df, ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[tv]], y = estimate)) +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$menses, color = "red") +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = meta$ov,     color = "forestgreen") +
    ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 1, color = "#0072B2") +
    ggplot2::geom_ribbon(ggplot2::aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
                         alpha = 0.2, fill = "#0072B2") +
    ggplot2::geom_point(data = max_pt, size = 2.5) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(data = min_pt, size = 2.5) +
    ggplot2::annotate("text", x = -1, y = Inf, label = ann,
                      hjust = -0.1, vjust = 1.5, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
    ggplot2::labs(title = dplyr::first(plot_df$outcome_label), x = "Cycle Phase", y = NULL) +
    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1), breaks = seq(-1, 1, by = 0.5),
                                labels = meta$labels) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
    ggplot2::theme(plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 10, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
}
```

```{r generate-explore-pdfs, results='asis'}
# =========================================================
# GENERATE PER-OUTCOME EXPLORATION PDF REPORTS
# =========================================================
# For each DRSP outcome, generates a landscape PDF containing:
#   1. Summary page: descriptive stats + threshold table
#   2. Significance probability plot (logistic curve)
#   3. Histogram of range by significance
#   4. Empirical + modeled probability plot
#   5. Individual GAM trajectory pages for qualifying IDs
#      (significant at p < .05 OR range >= 0.5)
# =========================================================

drsp_vars <- get_drsp_variables(gam_df)
cat("Found", length(drsp_vars), "DRSP variables to process\n")

# Thresholds for the exploration (includes .20 as requested)
explore_thresholds <- c(0.10, 0.20, 0.25, 0.36, 0.50, 0.75, 1.00)

# Plot layout for individual GAM pages
plots_per_col_exp <- 3
plots_per_row_exp <- 3
max_plots_per_page_exp <- plots_per_col_exp * plots_per_row_exp

# Table theme for summary pages
summary_table_theme <- gridExtra::ttheme_default(
  base_size = 11,
  core   = list(fg_params = list(hjust = 0, x = 0.05)),
  colhead = list(fg_params = list(hjust = 0, x = 0.05, fontface = "bold"))
)
threshold_table_theme <- gridExtra::ttheme_default(
  base_size = 10,
  core   = list(fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5)),
  colhead = list(fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5, fontface = "bold"))
)

for (target_var in drsp_vars) {
  cat(sprintf("\n=== Processing: %s ===\n", target_var))

  # 1. Run exploration analysis
  result <- tryCatch(
    analyze_one_outcome(gam_df, target_var,
                        thresholds = explore_thresholds,
                        n_bins = 10),
    error = function(e) {
      message("  Skipping ", target_var, ": ", e$message)
      NULL
    }
  )
  if (is.null(result)) next

  # 2. Identify qualifying IDs (significant OR range >= 0.5)
  qualifying_ids <- result$data %>%
    dplyr::filter(significant_num == 1 | range >= 0.5) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(range)) %>%
    dplyr::pull(id)

  # 3. Get outcome label
  outcome_label <- if (target_var %in% names(outcomes)) {
    outcomes[[target_var]]
  } else {
    target_var
  }

  cat(sprintf("  N participants: %d | N significant: %d | N qualifying IDs: %d\n",
              result$descriptives_overall$n_participants,
              result$descriptives_overall$n_significant,
              length(qualifying_ids)))

  # 4. Open PDF
  pdf_path <- file.path(gam_exp_folder, paste0("Explore_", target_var, ".pdf"))
  grDevices::pdf(pdf_path, width = 11, height = 8.5)

  # ---- PAGE 1: Summary + Threshold Table ----
  title_grob <- grid::textGrob(
    paste("GAM Cyclicity Exploration:", outcome_label),
    gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold")
  )
  subtitle_grob <- grid::textGrob(
    paste("Variable:", target_var),
    gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "italic")
  )

  desc_df <- data.frame(
    Metric = c("Total Participants",
               "N Significant (p < .05)",
               "Proportion Significant",
               "Median Range",
               "Mean Observations per ID"),
    Value = c(
      as.character(result$descriptives_overall$n_participants),
      as.character(result$descriptives_overall$n_significant),
      sprintf("%.1f%%", result$descriptives_overall$prop_significant * 100),
      sprintf("%.2f", median(result$data$range, na.rm = TRUE)),
      sprintf("%.1f", mean(result$data$n, na.rm = TRUE))
    ),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  desc_tbl <- gridExtra::tableGrob(desc_df, rows = NULL, theme = summary_table_theme)

  thresh_display <- result$threshold_table %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      threshold = sprintf("%.2f", threshold),
      n_at_or_above = as.character(n_at_or_above),
      prop_of_sample = sprintf("%.1f%%", prop_of_sample * 100),
      n_significant = as.character(n_significant),
      prop_significant = ifelse(is.na(prop_significant), "N/A",
                                sprintf("%.1f%%", prop_significant * 100))
    )
  names(thresh_display) <- c("Range Threshold", "N >= Threshold",
                             "% of Sample", "N Significant", "% Significant")

  thresh_tbl <- gridExtra::tableGrob(thresh_display, rows = NULL,
                                     theme = threshold_table_theme)

  thresh_header <- grid::textGrob(
    "Significance Rates at Range Thresholds",
    gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold")
  )

  note_grob <- grid::textGrob(
    sprintf("Individual GAM pages follow for %d qualifying IDs (significant OR range >= 0.5)",
            length(qualifying_ids)),
    gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 11, fontface = "italic")
  )

  gridExtra::grid.arrange(
    title_grob, subtitle_grob, desc_tbl, thresh_header, thresh_tbl, note_grob,
    ncol = 1,
    heights = grid::unit(c(0.10, 0.05, 0.22, 0.08, 0.40, 0.08), "npc")
  )

  # ---- PAGE 2: Significance Probability Plot ----
  print(result$plot_probability)

  # ---- PAGE 3: Histogram ----
  print(result$plot_histogram)

  # ---- PAGE 4: Empirical + Model ----
  print(result$plot_empirical)

  # ---- PAGES 5+: Individual GAM trajectory plots for qualifying IDs ----
  if (length(qualifying_ids) > 0 && length(time_vars) > 0) {
    tv <- time_vars[1]

    id_plots <- list()
    for (qid in qualifying_ids) {
      dat_id <- subset(df_raw, id == qid)
      if (nrow(dat_id) == 0) next

      res <- tryCatch(
        fit_outcome(dat_id, target_var, tv, outcomes),
        error = function(e) NULL
      )
      if (is.null(res)) next

      p <- create_annotated_plot(res$pred, tv, axis_meta)
      if (!is.null(p)) {
        id_range <- round(res$sig$range, 2)
        id_pval <- res$sig$p.value
        id_stars <- dplyr::case_when(
          is.finite(id_pval) & id_pval < 0.001 ~ "***",
          is.finite(id_pval) & id_pval < 0.01  ~ "**",
          is.finite(id_pval) & id_pval < 0.05  ~ "*",
          TRUE ~ ""
        )
        p <- p + ggplot2::labs(
          title = sprintf("ID %s %s (range=%.2f)", qid, id_stars, id_range)
        )
        id_plots[[length(id_plots) + 1]] <- p
      }
    }

    if (length(id_plots) > 0) {
      pages <- split(id_plots,
                     ceiling(seq_along(id_plots) / max_plots_per_page_exp))

      for (page_plots in pages) {
        if (length(page_plots) < max_plots_per_page_exp) {
          page_plots <- c(page_plots,
                         rep(list(patchwork::plot_spacer()),
                             max_plots_per_page_exp - length(page_plots)))
        }

        final_page <- patchwork::wrap_plots(plotlist = page_plots, ncol = plots_per_col_exp) +
          patchwork::plot_annotation(
            title = paste("Individual GAM Trajectories:", outcome_label),
            subtitle = paste("Time variable:", tv),
            theme = ggplot2::theme(
              plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
              plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(size = 11)
            )
          )
        print(final_page)
      }
    }
  }

  grDevices::dev.off()
  message(sprintf("  Saved: %s", pdf_path))
}

cat("\n=== All exploration PDFs generated ===\n")
cat("Output folder:", gam_exp_folder, "\n")
```
